title: The Nature of Seeds
wikitext:
[]: The Nature of Seeds             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 06, 2015 11:46 am Bumping this because I just read Bagheera's fanfic and it interests me. So little of this discussion has anything to do with the thread you bumped that I think it's better to just make it it's own thing. -NemZ The Seeds very much have free will. This is made evident by the fact the spears were created in the first place. You don't need something to make sure the Seeds don't go off script if they can't do it. Rei knew exactly what she was doing. She did it because she picked Shinji as her avatar for humanity, the only person she had a bond with. If he decided life wasn't worth living then it wasn't. You can see a bit of cynicism to this regard as she's tanging people saying the world is overflowing with sorrow. I suppose you could say she did it for the right reasons but it makes me wonder exactly what kind of selection process was used since Kaworu did almost the exact same thing. To me this is proof of why the spears needed to be made, proof of why the forbidden fusion was forbidden, proof that the FAR were indeed human. Capable of making poor choices based off their emotions. And when you hand something like that the kind of power they were handed you need something to watch them otherwise you're just asking for problems. Though clearly the safeguards failed spectacularly.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 06, 2015 12:34 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: I suppose you could say she did it for the right reasons but it makes me wonder exactly what kind of selection process was used since Kaworu did almost the exact same thing. To me this is proof of why the spears needed to be made, proof of why the forbidden fusion was forbidden, proof that the FAR were indeed human. Capable of making poor choices based off their emotions. And when you hand something like that the kind of power they were handed you need something to watch them otherwise you're just asking for problems. Though clearly the safeguards failed spectacularly.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 06, 2015 12:40 pm Eh I'd be more forgiving of that if the security procedure for Adam and Lilith landing on the same planet wasn't put one of them to sleep for 4 billion years. Adam didn't actually need a garden world like Lilith did, Adam could have just walked off the planet and been just fine. I feel they overlooked some very simple things that could have avoided the whole mess. But once again that makes them human.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 06, 2015 12:46 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Eh I'd be more forgiving of that if the security procedure for Adam and Lilith landing on the same planet wasn't put one of them to sleep for 4 billion years. Adam didn't actually need a garden world like Lilith did, Adam could have just walked off the planet and been just fine. I feel they overlooked some very simple things that could have avoided the whole mess. But once again that makes them human.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 06, 2015 12:51 pm Arael and Sahaquiel had no problems moving around in space. Sure it'd take a long time to reach another system but time is meaningless to this particular branch of humanity. This is, of course, drifting off topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 06, 2015 12:59 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Arael and Sahaquiel had no problems moving around in space. Sure it'd take a long time to reach another system but time is meaningless to this particular branch of humanity. This is, of course, drifting off topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 06, 2015 1:05 pm I mean I guess that's possible, free will and all, but I'd find it a bit odd if 2 people that began this quest to save humanity however many thousands of years prior to this event would be so dickish to one another especially when one is designed to produce humans that really don't care about their physical environment. It would reflect very poorly on them. But then I'm already questioning the selection because they both decided to use a single, very clearly emotionally compromised child as their avatar for humanity that they both shirked their duty over so who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 06, 2015 1:08 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: I mean I guess that's possible, free will and all, but I'd find it a bit odd if 2 people that began this quest to save humanity however many thousands of years prior to this event would be so dickish to one another especially when one is designed to produce humans that really don't care about their physical environment. It would reflect very poorly on them. But then I'm already questioning the selection because they both decided to use a single, very clearly emotionally compromised child as their avatar for humanity that they both shirked their duty over so who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 06, 2015 2:57 pm Hmm, going through the NGE2 threads again there was something about Lilith wanting to steal the FoL. That carries all sorts of unpleasant implications about the Seeds.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 06, 2015 7:11 pm Yeah, pretty crazy stuff regarding why, though Rei (2!) didn't let that happen in her Good End (merged with Lilith, destroyed her from the inside instead of starting up 3I).

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 06, 2015 7:41 pm What was the ultimate reason for that? I don't know if it's because my eyes glazed over or it just never got posted but I never did see the why.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 06, 2015 9:19 pm I think it was left vague, though even Rei 1* in Eva-00 (yep, it's her) tells Rei 2 to retrieve the Fruit of Life from Adam during Rei's Leliel events. See here , don't mind the mess. *Even, as in, it's usually Lilith in Rei 2's Scenario that bugs her about getting the FoL. One interpretation could be that, Lilith proper was power-hungry in some sense, so the Spears keeping the SoLs in check was good! Though, I prefer to think of Lilith as a mama bear that wanted to protect her kids/have Earth belong to Lilin, not Adam's Angels. Then again, I also like to think Lilith and Adam didn't necessarily hate each other--they were just in a bad situation--it was more the Lilin and Angels that caused problems.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 06, 2015 9:27 pm Either way would indicate a blatant willingness to screw each other over and abuse a massively dangerous technology that was declared forbidden for good reason. Really worrying traits to be displaying.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 06, 2015 9:34 pm I'm of the mind that Lilith wanted an S2 not because she was breaking the rules, but rather because she already had an S2 that was breaking down after 4 billion years of use and abuse. IMO Lilith and Adam are (or were at one point) functionally identical, with both having the fruits of life and wisdom. To me this is obvious because, on the one hand, Kaworu is a person with a mind (as alien as it might be), while Lilith lasted for four billion years -- impossible without a nigh-inexhaustible power source like an S2. If it walks like a duck, and quacks like a duck, well . . . So, in light of that Lilith's not quite as deranged as she might otherwise seem. Just keeping up appearances so she can look after her kids.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 06, 2015 9:40 pm I'm sure we've had this argument before. That would mean Lilith was created with the FoL and the FoK which is expressly indicated as being something you don't do. Now you want to say the ship had a power source that had long since broken down that's something else.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 06, 2015 9:47 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: I'm sure we've had this argument before. That would mean Lilith was created with the FoL and the FoK which is expressly indicated as being something you don't do. Now you want to say the ship had a power source that had long since broken down that's something else.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Apr 06, 2015 9:50 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: I'm sure we've had this argument before.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 06, 2015 9:53 pm Monk Ed wrote: Care to recount what makes it so clear? (And I sense a split perhaps sooner than later.)

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Apr 06, 2015 9:56 pm A rock can sit around for eons, doesn't mean it's tapped into an infinite energy source. For all we know Lilith is a corpse, and by the time we see her in the show she's hooked up to things that could be feeding her whatever she needs to do whatever it is they use her for. Bagheera wrote: Also, limitless LCL, which comes from the LCL sea, which is inside the S2 (per NGE2 and/or Chronicle, can't recall which).

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Apr 06, 2015 9:59 pm I think you're taking the FoK a bit too literally Bagheera. It isn't meant to say the Angels don't have minds, they obviously do. It's meant to say they lack a certain kind of understanding. Remember how fascinated Kaworu was with music? That's something the Angels would never do. As for why give Lilith a power source that functions similarly yet isn't the FoL, that's rather simple. So they don't make a being that is totally uncontrollable. It's entirely possible the ship had a power source for Lilith. It had all sorts of alien tech sitting around in it. It's possible it got damaged and stopped working because of the crash. This could very much make Lilith want to steal the FoL but that's still a blatant violation of the rules. Edit: This is what the CI had to say about LCL The white giant crucified in Terminal Dogma. It bore life such as apes and dolphins, and, as the final form, Lilin — that is, humankind. In addition, the liquid called LCL is Lilith's body fluid.
